name: node-safe-buffer
title: node-safe-buffer
description:
Safer Node.js Buffer API
The goal of this package is to provide a safe replacement for the node.js
`Buffer`. It's a drop-in replacement for `Buffer`. You can use it by adding
one `require` line to the top of your node.js modules; for example
var Buffer = require('safe-buffer').Buffer
Existing buffer code will continue to work without issues.
.
Node.js is an event-based server-side JavaScript engine.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use node-safe-buffer because Safer Node.js Buffer API.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use node-safe-buffer as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair node-safe-buffer with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `node-safe-buffer --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
